sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a complaint of back pain and tingling sensation in the right leg, which started after raking leaves in the yard.
The patient reports that the pain was fine when they were raking leaves, but worsened after being indoors for 2 hours.
The patient has had similar pain in the past, which resolved on its own after a day.
Past medical history: The patient reports a past episode of back pain 3 years ago that resolved on its own.
The patient's partner reports that the patient normally does not engage in physical activity, but has been doing yard work lately.
The patient and partner have a high pain tolerance and do not usually complain about pain.
Review of systems: The patient reports tingling sensation in the right leg, but no other symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature were not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has some pain on flexion, no pain on extension, and pain to palpation around the L5.
The patient's partner reports that the patient's pain worsens when standing up.
The patient's dorsiflexion is normal, and pulses are equal in all extremities.
Test Results: The patient had a normal x-ray of the low back.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely sprained their low back, as evidenced by the normal x-ray and physical exam findings.
Problem list: Lower back sprain; suspected muscle strain; confirmed lumbar pain; [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg PO q 12 hours for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help with stretches and exercises to improve the patient's flexibility and strength.
I want the patient to rest and avoid heavy lifting or bending for the next 2 weeks.
If the patient's symptoms do not improve, I will consider ordering an MRI.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in 2 weeks to re-evaluate their symptoms and consider further testing, such as an MRI, if necessary.
